features.name: ACID Compliance & Transactions
features.description: Guarantees strong transactional consistency and integrity for relational schemas across zonal or global deployments.

features.name: Automated Backups & PITR
features.description: Enables point-in-time recovery (PITR) to restore database states back to a specific millisecond in case of corruption.

features.name: High Availability Failover
features.description: Deploys active-passive synchronous replication across zones, executing automatic failover during outages.

iamRoles.description: Full access to administer Cloud SQL instances, backups, and user credentials.
Full administrative permissions over Cloud Spanner Firestore instances, schemas, and queries.

cliExamples.description: List all read replicas attached to database instances

design: Relational databases are chosen when workloads require full ACID transactional guarantees and complex table joins.
Cloud SQL supports standard MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server, scaling vertically through custom machine types.
Cloud Spanner is designed for global relational scalability, offering horizontal writes and transactional consistency.
For Cloud Spanner, primary key design must avoid sequential values (like timestamps) to prevent partition hotspots.
AlloyDB is a PostgreSQL-compatible analytical and transactional database built for high-performance enterprise engines.
Database schema migrations require executing Schema Conversion Tool and Database Migration Service (DMS).
Read replicas offload read-heavy dashboard query traffic from the primary transactional instance.
Use database connection pools to prevent exhausting database thread connection limits.
security: Connect securely to Cloud SQL using the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy, which whitelists connections without public IPs.
Database IAM Authentication allows users and service accounts to login using Google credentials instead of static passwords.
Enforce private IP configurations using Serverless VPC Access or Private Service Connect for database interaction.
Always encrypt database backups and transaction logs using Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) via KMS.
VPC Service Controls protect databases from data exfiltration by creating a secure network boundary.
Enable SSL/TLS configurations on client connection strings to secure data in transit.
Database administration actions (e.g. changing root password, backups) are audited in Cloud Audit Logs.
Implement row-level and column-level database security to restrict unauthorized service accounts from reading sensitive tables.
reliability: HA configurations use synchronous replication to a standby instance in a different zone in the same region.
Standard read replicas replicate asynchronously, which may lead to replication lag during high write spikes.
Point-in-time recovery (PITR) uses write-ahead logging (WAL) to enable restores to any timestamp within the log retention window.
Perform regular automated daily backups, setting retention policies to match regulatory compliance needs.
Cross-region replicas provide disaster recovery, enabling prompt promote-to-primary actions during region failure.
Cloud Spanner provides an availability SLA of 99.999% for multi-regional instances using Paxos-based replication.
Establish monitoring metrics on database CPU usage, memory usage, and storage growth rate.
Auto-resize storage triggers disk expansion automatically, preventing database crashes due to full disks.
cost: Relational databases charge for provisioned compute (vCPUs/RAM), database storage (GB/month), and backup storage.
Read replicas are billed as full independent database instances, increasing compute cost.
Storage is billed on provisioned capacity, not active database contents; size disks carefully.
Configure storage auto-resize with a safety threshold to prevent runaway disk storage billing.
Use committed use discounts (CUDs) for database instances running continuously in production environments.
Schedule development database shutdowns (e.g., stopping non-HA instances overnight) to minimize off-hour billing.
Review Recommender suggestions to downsize database instances with low historical CPU and memory utilization.
Prune old, unused database snapshots and test backups to minimize storage cost leaks.
